question:
Directions:Answer the questions based on the

information given below.

There are two types of flats i.e., X and Y on each

floor of a five- floor building. The bottommost

floor is numbered as 1 and the topmost floor is

numbered as 5. Flat X is in west of Flat Y. Ten

persons (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, and J) live in these

flats such that one person lives in each flat. All

flats are of same dimensions.

Note: If it is given that ‘a person’ lives adjacent to

‘another person’ then both persons live on the

same floor unless stated otherwise. If it is given

that ‘a persons’ lives one/two floors above/below

‘another person’ then both persons live in the

same type of flat unless stated otherwise.

J lives one floor below I. G lives two floors below

D, but not in the same type of flat. I lives in Flat X.

H, who lives adjacent to A, lives two floors above
question_html:
Directions:Answer the questions based on the

information given below.

There are two types of flats i.e., X and Y on each

floor of a five- floor building. The bottommost

floor is numbered as 1 and the topmost floor is

numbered as 5. Flat X is in west of Flat Y. Ten

persons (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, and J) live in these

flats such that one person lives in each flat. All

flats are of same dimensions.

Note: If it is given that ‘a person’ lives adjacent to

‘another person’ then both persons live on the

same floor unless stated otherwise. If it is given

that ‘a persons’ lives one/two floors above/below

‘another person’ then both persons live in the

same type of flat unless stated otherwise.

J lives one floor below I. G lives two floors below

D, but not in the same type of flat. I lives in Flat X.

H, who lives adjacent to A, lives two floors above
questionHtml:
Directions:Answer the questions based on the

information given below.

There are two types of flats i.e., X and Y on each

floor of a five- floor building. The bottommost

floor is numbered as 1 and the topmost floor is

numbered as 5. Flat X is in west of Flat Y. Ten

persons (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, and J) live in these

flats such that one person lives in each flat. All

flats are of same dimensions.

Note: If it is given that ‘a person’ lives adjacent to

‘another person’ then both persons live on the

same floor unless stated otherwise. If it is given

that ‘a persons’ lives one/two floors above/below

‘another person’ then both persons live in the

same type of flat unless stated otherwise.

J lives one floor below I. G lives two floors below

D, but not in the same type of flat. I lives in Flat X.

H, who lives adjacent to A, lives two floors above

options.text:
C and I live in the same type of flat. E lives one

floor above F. B doesn’t live adjacent to J.

B lives _____ floors above A.
options.html:
C and I live in the same type of flat. E lives one

floor above F. B doesn’t live adjacent to J.

B lives _____ floors above A.

optionsHtml:
C and I live in the same type of flat. E lives one

floor above F. B doesn’t live adjacent to J.

B lives _____ floors above A.
explanation:
Starting Point: Start with placing J, I, H, C, and A

to form least number of cases initially.

Clues:

1. I lives in Flat X.

2. J lives one floor below I. C and I live in the

same type of flat.

3. H, who lives adjacent to A, lives two floors

above C.

Inferences:

From clue 1, clue 2, and clue 3, we get I lives in

Flat X of either 5 or 2
 floor. J lives in Flat X of

either 4 or 1 floor. H lives in Flat X of either 3

or 5 floor. C lives in Flat X of either 1 or 3

floor.

Case I: When I lives in Flat X of 5 floor.

Case II: When I lives in Flat X of 2
 floor.

Clues:

1. G lives two floors below D, but not in the same

type of flat.

2. E lives one floor above F.

Inferences:

From clue 1 and clue 2, we get D lives either in

Flat Y or Flat X of 4 floor. E, F, and B lives in Flat

Y. B lives adjacent to either I or J. E lives adjacent

to either G or D. F lives adjacent to C.

Case I:

Case II:

options.text:
Each flat of one floor is above that of another

floor. (such that flat A of floor 1 is immediately

below flat A of floor 2, and so on)

Seven friends - Rini, Ram, Rancho, Raju, Riya, Raj

and Rinky, live in those flats such that no flat is

empty. Rinky lives on the top floor. Raj lives in flat

A, but not on first floor and, he lives alone. Rini

lives in the flat immediately below Riya's flat.

Ram and Riya live on odd numbered floors.

Rancho lives to east of Ram. Raju lives on even

numbered floor, but not immediately below

Rinky's & Raj's flat.

Find the odd pair.
options.html:

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions.

Six people A, B, C, D, E and F live in a three-

floored building numbered 1 - 3 from bottom to

top. The building has two flats on each floor

named X and Y such that flat X is to the west of

flat Y. Everyone owns a different car viz. Fiat,

Ford, Honda, Kia, Skoda, and Tata are not

necessarily in the similar order. D and the one

who owns Tata lives in the same-named flat. E

who owns Skoda does not live on the same floor

as D. A lives on a floor somewhere above C.

Neither D nor A owns Fiat. The one who owns Kia

lives in a flat to the east of B's flat. The one who

owns Tata lives in a flat to the south-west of the

one who owns Kia. B lives on an even-numbered

floor. F and E live on the same floor. The one who

owns Honda and Kia live in different flats.

Four of the following are related to each other in

some way and thus form a group, which of the

following does not belong to that group?
question_html:

answer the questions.

flat Y. Everyone owns a different car viz. Fiat,

who owns Skoda does not live on the same floor

as D. A lives on a floor somewhere above C.

Four of the following are related to each other in

some way and thus form a group, which of the

following does not belong to that group?
explanation:
It is given that,

Six people are A, B, C, D, E, and F.

The six cars are Fiat, Ford, Honda, Kia, Skoda, and

Tata.

B lives on an even-numbered floor.

The one who owns Kia lives in a flat to the east of

B's flat.

The one who owns Tata lives in a flat to the

south-west of the one who owns Kia.

We get,

 X
Y

2 B
Kia

1 Tata 

D and the one who owns Tata lives in the same-

named flat.

E who owns Skoda does not live on the same

floor as D.

F and E live on the same floor.

We get,

Y

Kia

1 F, Tata E, Skoda

A lives on a floor somewhere above C.

Neither D nor A owns Fiat.

The one who own Honda and Kia live in different

flats.

Hence, D - Honda, A - Ford.

The final arrangement is,

 X

C, Kia

E, Skoda

Flat X.

There are two types of flats i.e., X and Y on each

textPlain:
Answer the questions based on the information

given below.

question:
Answer the questions based on the information

given below:

Eight persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H live in a

building, which consists of two types of flats - P

and Q in the building, such that each type of flat

consists of four floors. The bottommost floor is

numbered as 1 and the topmost floor is

numbered as 4, All floors are of same dimension.

Flat P is to the west of flat Q. Each of them likes

different laptops- HP, Nokia, Dell, HCL, Asus,

Apple, Samsung and Lenovo but not necessarily

in the same order.

Note: If a person lives one/two/three floors

above/below another person then, they live in

the same type of flat unless stated otherwise. If a

person lives adjacent to another person, then

they live on the same floor but in different types

of flat.

A lives adjacent to H who likes Nokia. E who likes

Lenovo lives in an even numbered floor. F lives

adjacent to E. C who likes Apple, doesn't live in

flat P. A neither likes Asus nor HP. D lives one

floor below A. A lives on a floor below G, who

likes Samsung (either in same or in different type

of flats). The one who likes Dell, lives one floor

below the one, who likes Asus. B who lives in flat

Q, lives one floor below F.

F lives above the one, who likes _____ laptop (in

the same type of flat).

the same type of flat).

Eight persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H live in a

building, which consists of two types of flats - P

and Q in the building, such that each type of flat

consists of four floors. The bottommost floor is

numbered as 1 and the topmost floor is

numbered as 4, All floors are of same dimension.

Flat P is to the west of flat Q. Each of them likes

different laptops- HP, Nokia, Dell, HCL, Asus,

Apple, Samsung and Lenovo but not necessarily

in the same order.

Note: If a person lives one/two/three floors

above/below another person then, they live in

the same type of flat unless stated otherwise. If a

person lives adjacent to another person, then

they live on the same floor but in different types

of flat.

A lives adjacent to H who likes Nokia. E who likes

Lenovo lives in an even numbered floor. F lives

adjacent to E. C who likes Apple, doesn't live in

flat P. A neither likes Asus nor HP. D lives one

floor below A. A lives on a floor below G, who

likes Samsung (either in same or in different type

of flats). The one who likes Dell, lives one floor

below the one, who likes Asus. B who lives in flat

Q, lives one floor below F.

Who lives on the second floor of flat P?
explanation:
E who likes Lenovo, lives in an even numbered

floor. F lives adjacent to E. B who lives in flat Q,

lives one floor below F.

textPlain:
Answer the questions based on the information

given below:

Eight persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H live in a

building, which consists of two types of flats - P

and Q in the building, such that each type of flat

consists of four floors. The bottommost floor is

numbered as 1 and the topmost floor is

numbered as 4, All floors are of same dimension.

Flat P is to the west of flat Q. Each of them likes

different laptops- HP, Nokia, Dell, HCL, Asus,

Apple, Samsung and Lenovo but not necessarily

in the same order.

Note: If a person lives one/two/three floors

above/below another person then, they live in

the same type of flat unless stated otherwise. If a

person lives adjacent to another person, then

they live on the same floor but in different types

of flat.

A lives adjacent to H who likes Nokia. E who likes

Lenovo lives in an even numbered floor. F lives

adjacent to E. C who likes Apple, doesn't live in

flat P. A neither likes Asus nor HP. D lives one

floor below A. A lives on a floor below G, who

likes Samsung (either in same or in different type

of flats). The one who likes Dell, lives one floor

below the one, who likes Asus. B who lives in flat

Q, lives one floor below F.

Who lives on the second floor of flat P?

question:
G lives two floors below D, but not in the same

type of flat.

questionHtml:

explanation:
Starting Point: Start with placing J, I, H, C, and A

to form least number of cases initially.

Clues:

1. I lives in Flat X.

2. J lives one floor below I. C and I live in the

same type of flat.

3. H, who lives adjacent to A, lives two floors

above C.

Inferences:

From clue 1, clue 2, and clue 3, we get I lives in

Flat X of either 5 or 2
 floor. J lives in Flat X of

either 4 or 1 floor. H lives in Flat X of either 3

or 5 floor. C lives in Flat X of either 1 or 3

floor.

Case I: When I lives in Flat X of 5 floor.

Case II: When I lives in Flat X of 2
 floor.
